title: phyllanthales
intro: The phyllanthales is a botanical order that comprises a diverse group of flowering plants, typically characterized by their small, often globular fruits and varied leaf shapes. This order includes approximately 55 families, such as the Euphorbiaceae, which contains the poinsettia, and the Cyperaceae, which comprises sedges and rushes. The Orchidaceae family, one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants, is also part of the phyllanthales order. However, not all members of the phyllanthales order are as well-known as these examples, with many species found in tropical and subtropical regions.

families.family_desc: Phyllanthaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the leaf flowers, comprising approximately 1,000 species of herbs, shrubs, and trees.
